title: Nephrodium erubescens
taxonomy: Nephrodium erubescens belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. It is classified under the class Polypodiopsida, which is a subclass of the division Pteridophyta. The order is Polypodiales, and the family is Dryopteridaceae. This plant is further classified into the genus Nephrodium, which is a type of fern. The specific classification of Nephrodium erubescens is: Nephrodium erubescens (now known as Elaphoglossum erubescens), is classified under the family Dryopteridaceae, genus Elaphoglossum.
uses: Nephrodium erubescens, also known as maidenhair fern, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional Asian medicine, the fern has been used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Its potential antimicrobial properties have also been explored for treating infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Nephrodium erubescens in modern medicine.
